# Faultsense

> Faultsense is a lightweight (6.5KB gzipped) browser agent that validates feature correctness in production through declarative HTML attribute assertions. Think E2E test assertions, but running against real user sessions.

- Docs: https://www.faultsense.org/docs
- Full reference: [llms-full.txt](./llms-full.txt)
- Examples: https://www.faultsense.org/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/Fault-Sense/faultsense-agent

## How It Works

Add `fs-*` HTML attributes to the element a user interacts with. The attributes declare: what event triggers the assertion, what DOM or network outcome is expected, and how to verify it. The agent monitors real user sessions and reports pass/fail results to a collector.

## Attribute Reference

### Required

- `fs-assert="<key>"` — Assertion key. Use `/` to group: `"checkout/add-to-cart"`, `"profile/media/upload-photo"`
- `fs-trigger="<value>"` — One of: `click`, `dblclick`, `change`, `blur`, `submit`, `mount`, `unmount`, `load`, `error`

### Assertion Types (at least 1 required)

Value is a CSS selector, optionally with inline modifiers in brackets:

- `fs-assert-added="<selector>"` — Element added to DOM
- `fs-assert-removed="<selector>"` — Element removed from DOM
- `fs-assert-updated="<selector>"` — Element or subtree mutated
- `fs-assert-visible="<selector>"` — Element exists and visible
- `fs-assert-hidden="<selector>"` — Element exists but hidden
- `fs-assert-loaded="<selector>"` — Media element finished loading

Response-conditional — append status to type name:
- `fs-assert-{type}-{status}="<selector>"` — e.g., `fs-assert-added-200=".success"`, `fs-assert-removed-4xx=".error"`
- Status: exact (200, 404) or range (2xx, 4xx, 5xx). Exact takes priority.
- Multiple conditions per element create independent assertions. When one matches, siblings are dismissed.

### Inline Modifiers (in the value)

Chained CSS-like brackets after the selector:

- `[text-matches=pattern]` — Text content regex/string match
- `[classlist=active:true,hidden:false]` — Class presence check
- `[attr=value]` — Any other key is an attribute check

Example: `fs-assert-updated='#count[text-matches=\d+]'`

### Element-Level Attributes

- `fs-assert-timeout="<ms>"` — Override default timeout (default: 1000ms)
- `fs-assert-mpa="true"` — Persist assertion across page navigation

### Network Association

Response-conditional assertions require linking via `fs-resp-for`:
- Request header: `fetch(url, { headers: { "fs-resp-for": "<assertion-key>" } })`
- Response header: Server sets `fs-resp-for: <assertion-key>`
- Query param: `fetch(url + "?fs-resp-for=<assertion-key>")`

### Assertion Key Convention

Use `/` separators to group assertions hierarchically. Nest as deep as needed:

```
fs-assert="checkout/add-to-cart"
fs-assert="checkout/submit-order"
fs-assert="profile/media/upload-photo"
fs-assert="auth/login"
```

Keys must be stable across releases. Human-readable labels are configured on the collector side, not in the HTML.

## Placement Rule

Attributes go on the element that receives the user event. Only the exact `event.target` is processed.

## Examples

### Button click updates DOM element

```html
<button
  fs-assert="counter/increment"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated='#count[text-matches=Count: [1-9]\d*]'>
  Increment
</button>
<span id="count">Count: 0</span>
```

### Form with response-conditional assertions

```html
<form
  fs-assert="contact/submit-form"
  fs-trigger="submit"
  fs-assert-added-201=".success-msg"
  fs-assert-added-4xx=".error-msg"
  fs-assert-timeout="2000">
  <input name="email" type="email" />
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

### Initial state validation on page load

```html
<div
  fs-assert="todo/initial-state"
  fs-trigger="mount"
  fs-assert-visible=".todos-empty">
  <!-- app content -->
</div>
```
